front: Opera: almost always performed on a double bill with Leoncavallo's Pagliacci (the 'Cav/Pag' bill); often considered the first verismo opera
Opera: set entirely in the main square of a Sicilian village on Easter morning; based on a short story by Giovanni Verga
Opera: won Edoardo Sonzogno's one-act opera competition in 1890, making its composer instantly famous
Opera: protagonist bites his rival's ear to accept a duel challenge, then begs his mother for 'one kiss, Mother!' before the fight
Opera: protagonist returns from military service to find his fiancée Lola has married the teamster Alfio; seduces Santuzza to make Lola jealous
Opera: a party beginning with a character singing 'Hail to the bubbling wine' ends with him biting his rival's ear
Opera: the teamster Alfio sings the aria 'Il cavallo scalpita' while cracking his whip, claiming snow and rain don't matter because his wife waits at home
Opera: aria 'Voi lo sapete' sung by Santuzza to Mamma Lucia, revealing Turiddu's adultery
work: Cavalleria rusticana – Arias and Musical Moments

front: Opera: the church chorus sings the Easter hymn Regina Coeli; a character barred from the church sings 'Voi lo sapete' outside
work: Cavalleria rusticana – Arias and Musical Moments

front: Opera: during the prelude, the lead tenor sings an offstage love serenade/siciliana
work: Cavalleria rusticana – Arias and Musical Moments

front: Opera: intermezzo plays during the opening (and closing) credits of Raging Bull (dir. Martin Scorsese)
Opera: intermezzo plays in The Godfather Part III when Michael Corleone screams clutching his dead daughter Mary's body
Opera: its celebrated intermezzo accompanies the deserted town square during Easter (while villagers are in church) and is frequently excerpted as a concert piece
